title: Hopes for reopening the Strait of Hormuz push Asian shares higher, as oil prices hold above $100
summary: Asian shares jumped on Thursday, with Tokyo's Nikkei 225 gaining 5.7% to a record intraday high as investors waited to see if the U.S. and Iran will strike a deal allowing tankers to deliver crude from the Persian Gulf again.  Japan's benchmark Nikkei 225 index jumped 3,402 points to 62,915.87 as markets in Tokyo reopened following “Golden Week” holidays.

title: Review & Preview: The Road Back to 50K
summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average finally joined the Nasdaq Composite and S&P 500 in exiting correction territory, but it ended the day just short of a major milestone.  The Dow rose 612 points, or 1.2% after briefly crossing the symbolic 50,000 level.  The S&P 500 ended up 1.5% while the Nasdaq jumped 2%.
